steps.title: Pin down the claim
steps.content: The underlined sentence says "Thanks to ‘Aha Pūnana Leo, the number of young people who speak the language has increased." Support must show (1) a rise in young Hawaiian speakers and (2) a connection to ‘Aha Pūnana Leo.

steps.title: Find the supporting evidence
steps.content: The correct answer provides a then-and-now count tied directly to the school: fewer than fifty children spoke Hawaiian at founding, versus more than 2,000 students at ‘Aha Pūnana Leo today. That is exactly the increase the claim asserts.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content:
• Choice A — Fewer than fifty children could speak Hawaiian when 'Aha Pūnana Leo was founded, but now more than 2,000 students at 'Aha Pūnana Leo speak it.: shows both the increase and its tie to the school. ✓
  • Choice B — Roughly 680,000 Native Hawaiian people lived in the United States in 2020, and a little less than half of them lived in Hawai'i.: a population statistic that says nothing about young Hawaiian speakers or the school. ✗
  • Choice C — Hawaiian is very similar to other languages that are spoken on the Polynesian Islands of the Pacific Ocean, including Tahitian, Samoan, and Māori.: linguistic kinship is irrelevant to whether speaker numbers rose. ✗
  • Choice D — Besides Native Hawaiians, tens of thousands of people from other Pacific Islander communities live in Hawai'i today, including over 37,000 Samoans.: about other Pacific Islander communities, not Hawaiian-speaking youth. ✗
That is the correct answer.
